seoTitle: Pull a Report of Emails Sent in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Find out how to generate a report showing emails sent from QuickBooks Online to track invoices, statements, and customer communication.
intro: Knowing which emails have gone out to clients, like invoices and statements, helps you follow up effectively and improve customer service. This guide covers how to pull a report of sent emails in QuickBooks Online.

steps.heading: Navigate to the reporting area
steps.body: Open the Reports section in QuickBooks Online and look for options related to sent transactions or email activity.

steps.heading: Filter the email data
steps.body: Apply filters by date range or customer to narrow down the report to the specific emails you want to review.

steps.heading: Review the report
steps.body: Check which invoices or statements were emailed and to whom, giving you insight into your communication history.

steps.heading: Use the data for follow-ups
steps.body: Reference the report to identify clients who may need a reminder or a resend of an important document.

faq.question: Can I see if a customer opened the email?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online can show when a document was sent, though detailed open-tracking may depend on your plan or integrated tools.

faq.question: Can this be automated?
faq.answer: Yes, automation features and integrations can help schedule reminders and track email activity without manual checking.

faq.question: Why would I need this report?
faq.answer: It's useful for confirming that invoices reached clients, identifying who needs a follow-up, and keeping records for accountability.

faq.question: Is this available to all QuickBooks Online users?
faq.answer: Basic email tracking and reporting is generally available across QuickBooks Online plans, though features can vary by subscription level.
